paragraphs.en: The Ming Tombs, located in a tranquil valley at the foot of Tianshou Mountain about 50 kilometers north of Beijing, are the final resting place of 13 emperors of the Ming Dynasty. The site was carefully chosen according to feng shui principles, with mountains forming a natural protective embrace.
paragraphs.zh: 明十三陵位于北京以北约50公里的天寿山麓一处幽静的山谷中，是明朝13位皇帝的陵寝所在地。陵址依据风水理论精心选定，群山环抱，形成天然的屏障。

paragraphs.en: The Sacred Way, a 7-kilometer path lined with 24 stone animal and 12 human statues, leads to the entrance of the tomb complex. Walking along this impressive avenue, visitors pass pairs of lions, camels, elephants, and mythical beasts, all standing as eternal guardians of the imperial tombs.
paragraphs.zh: 神道是一条7公里长的道路，两侧排列着24尊石兽和12尊石人像，通向陵区入口。沿着这条气势恢宏的大道前行，游客会经过成对的狮子、骆驼、大象和传说中的神兽，它们都是皇陵永恒的守护者。

paragraphs.en: The Dingling Tomb, the underground palace of Emperor Wanli, is the only Ming tomb to have been fully excavated by archaeologists. Discovered in 1956, its underground chambers revealed a vast treasure trove of gold, jade, porcelain, and silk that illuminated the lavish lifestyle of Ming royalty.
paragraphs.zh: 定陵是明神宗万历皇帝的地下宫殿，也是唯一一座经过考古完整发掘的明陵。1956年发掘后，其地宫出土了大量的金器、玉器、瓷器和丝织品，揭示了明朝皇室的奢华生活。

paragraphs.en: The Changling Tomb, the largest and oldest of the Ming Tombs, belongs to Emperor Yongle, the third emperor of the Ming Dynasty who moved the capital to Beijing and commissioned the Forbidden City. Its Hall of Eminent Favor, built entirely of nanmu wood, is one of the finest surviving Ming dynasty wooden structures.
paragraphs.zh: 长陵是十三陵中规模最大、年代最久的一座，属于明朝第三位皇帝永乐帝——正是他迁都北京并下令修建了紫禁城。其祾恩殿全部由楠木建成，是现存最精美的明代木结构建筑之一。

paragraphs.en: In 2003, the Ming Tombs were inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, recognized as an outstanding example of imperial funerary architecture integrated harmoniously with the natural landscape. Today, visitors can explore the site and gain a deeper understanding of China's rich imperial history.
paragraphs.zh: 2003年，明十三陵被联合国教科文组织列入世界文化遗产名录，被誉为皇家陵寝建筑与自然山水和谐融合的杰出典范。如今，游客可以实地探访，深入了解中国丰富的皇家历史文化。
